channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How a week of weather extremes upended the lives of millions of Americans
item.description: It was a week of fire and ice. It began with millions of people across the U.S. shivering amid blizzard conditions and frigid air that lasted for days, thanks to a jet stream that slips out of its usual path more often these days.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Californians scrambled to evacuate dogs, cats, horses, even pigs as LA flames closed in
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Arianna Buturovic kept a wary eye on distant smoke from the rescue shelter she runs outside Los Angeles for dogs at risk of being euthanized. Within hours, nearby mountains were ablaze and fire began encircling her.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and LESLIE AMBRIZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 18 migrants found stowed in tanker truck near San Antonio
item.description: A group of 18 migrants were found inside a tanker truck by Texas law enforcement hundreds of miles from the border in Guadalupe County, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: LA wildfires destroy numerous houses of worship. Clergy and congregants vow to persevere
item.description: Among the thousands of buildings destroyed in the Los Angeles wildfires were a mosque, a synagogue, a Catholic parish and a half-dozen Protestant churches.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Luis Andres Henao And Peter Smith, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Americans have dimmer view of Biden than they did of Trump or Obama as term ends, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As Joe Biden prepares to leave office, Americans have a dimmer view of his presidency than they did at the end of Donald Trump's first term or Barack Obama's second, a new poll finds. Around one-quarter of U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Polymarket allowing users to bet on deadly LA wildfires
item.description: An American prediction market platform is allowing users to place bets on deadly wildfires in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Anita Bryant, anti-gay rights activist and singer, dead at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant Dry, known popularly as Anita Bryant — a singer and crusader against the progress of gay rights — has died at the age of 84, her family announced in an obituary.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In Los Angeles, people snap up air filters and wear masks against smoke pollution
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — With levels of sooty air pollution far too high across swaths of the Los Angeles area, Dana Benton is sleeping with a mask on, even with her air purifier running.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York. The court’s order clears the way for Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Far from wildfire flames, residents of sunny Los Angeles go about their lives in disquiet and fear
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Pedestrians shuffled by the famed Chateau Marmont hotel, customers queued up at Starbucks on Sunset Boulevard and car horns bleated at gridlocked intersections.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A powerful winter storm's heavy snow and sleet cancels flights and closes schools across US South
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A powerful winter storm brought heavy snow and icy conditions to parts of the U.S. South on Thursday, forcing officials to close schools, cancel flights and warn residents in some of the worst-hit areas to stay off roads as it lumbered eastward through Oklahoma and Texas. Arkansas Gov.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and KENDRIA LAFLEUR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Las Vegas Raider Charles Snowden thought he was in Florida during DUI arrest: Video
item.description: Tests showed Snowden’s blood-alcohol level was more than double the legal limit after officers found him asleep and unresponsive in a running SUV, documents show.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Watchdog groups seeks investigation into RFK Jr. for voting from a 'sham' address
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York election officials are being urged to investigate whether Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Laken Riley Act overwhelmingly clears first hurdle in Senate with help from Democrats
item.description: The Senate on Thursday overwhelmingly cleared a key procedural hurdle toward passing the Laken Riley Act, a bill aimed at curbing crime by migrants, in the first vote of the year by the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Eaton Fire victim dies holding a hose, defending home
item.description: According to his loved ones, 66-year-old Victor Shaw died trying to defend the home that had been in his family for nearly 55 years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: PowerSchool hack exposes Indiana students' data
item.description: BROWNSBURG, Ind. -- Multiple Indianapolis area school corporations confirmed an unauthorized user accessed its student information system as part of a national data breach.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Haughn, NewsNation

item.title: US warns of risk from most of world’s online pharmacies
item.description: The office of the U.S. Trade Representative warned that there’s a risk of ineffective or dangerous drugs from online pharmacies that are being illegally run.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: California fire victim dies holding hose, defending home
item.description: One of the five confirmed fatalities from the destructive Eaton Fire was identified on Wednesday morning when his family found his body on the side of the road by their home – with a garden hose still in his hand.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How to safely evacuate a wildfire
item.description: Evacuating a wildfire area can be scary, but experts say people can significantly improve their own safety with a little preparation and common sense.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NY top court denies delaying Trump sentencing
item.description: New York’s high court on Thursday declined to halt President-elect Trump’s sentencing on his hush money criminal conviction, which is scheduled for Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: LIVE: More evacuations ordered after fast-moving Kenneth fire ignites in Los Angeles area
item.description: Firefighters are racing to contain a new wildfire that broke out close to homes in the area of Calabasas and Hidden Hills, northwest of Los Angeles, on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter will be honored at Washington funeral before burial in Georgia hometown
item.description: Jimmy Carter, who considered himself an outsider even as he sat in the Oval Office as the 39th U.S. president, will be honored Thursday with the pageantry of a funeral at Washington National Cathedral before a second service and burial in his tiny Georgia hometown.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Celebrity homes among those lost in devastating Los Angeles fires
item.description: Wildfires that are burning in and around Los Angeles have burned the homes of several celebrities, including Billy Crystal, Mandy Moore and Paris Hilton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: WATCH: Reporter douses flames endangering California home on live TV
item.description: While covering one of the multiple fires currently ablaze in California, one reporter — a former EMT and volunteer firefighter — stepped in to stop flames from spreading to a home.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, ABC 4

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WGN 9

item.title: US will keep protecting more than 2,000 Rocky Mountain grizzly bears
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Biden administration said Wednesday it will continue protecting about 2,000 grizzly bears in the Rocky Mountains despite opposition from Republican-led states. Federal officials also said they would ease some restrictions as conflicts between bears and humans rise.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana man killed girlfriend's 4-month-old puppy: Docs
item.description: An Indiana man faces a felony charge after police say he threw his girlfriend's dog onto the ground in frustration, killing the 4-month-old puppy.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis, longtime Newsom rival, offers help to California amid wildfires
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) offered assistance to California on Wednesday as the state grapples with severe wildfires in the greater Los Angeles area. “Our prayers are with everyone affected by the horrific fires in Southern California,” DeSantis said in a post on social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter lies in state at Capitol Rotunda, Trump among those paying respect
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will lie in state for a second day Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At last, some welcome news on college costs. Tuition has fallen significantly at many schools
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The cost of college keeps spiraling ever higher, right? Not necessarily. New research indicates students are paying significantly less to attend public universities than they were a decade ago. And tuition increases at private colleges have finally slowed after years of hefty rises.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY and CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden says he could've beaten Trump in November
item.description: President Joe Biden said early Wednesday that he thinks he would have beaten President-elect Donald Trump if he stuck with his reelection bid in an interview with USA Today.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington on Tuesday for state funeral rites that featured the kind of bipartisan praise and ceremonial pomp the Georgia Democrat rarely enjoyed at his political peak.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Palisades Fire in Los Angeles scorches 2,921 acres; many homes destroyed
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Tens of thousands of residents in the western Los Angeles area were under mandatory evacuation orders Tuesday evening as a wind-driven wildfire exploded in size and swept through communities in the Pacific Palisades. The Palisades Fire was first reported around 10:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5
